stemHtml: Regarding airflow around a wing, there are several specific points which can be defined. One of these is the separation point, which is where the...

options.a: boundary layer breaks down and disappears due to the turbulent flow that follows behind the separation point.
options.b: velocity of the boundary layer becomes zero at the surface, and after which the boundary layer detaches from the wing surface.
options.c: boundary layer becomes turbulent and thickens, and this point will move forward along the upper surface if the angle of attack is reduced.
options.d: laminar airflow becomes turbulent, which creates a stall due to the rapid increase of the adverse pressure gradient.
